author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
Ambrose Teaches with What Eye Christ Turned and Looked Upon Peter.
title: Ambrose Teaches with What Eye Christ Turned and Looked Upon Peter.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Chapter 49 [XLV.]—Ambrose Teaches with What Eye Christ Turned and Looked Upon Peter.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: That repentance, indeed, itself, which beyond all doubt is an action of the will, is wrought into action by the mercy and help of the Lord, is asserted by the blessed Ambrose in the following passage in the ninth book of the same work: “Good, says he, “are the tears which wash away sin.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: They upon whom the Lord at last turns and looks, bewail.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Peter denied Him first, and did not weep, because the Lord had not turned and looked upon him.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: He denied Him a second time, and still wept not, because the Lord had not even yet turned and looked upon him.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: The third time also he denied Him, Jesus turned and looked, and then he wept most bitterly.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Let these persons read the Gospel; let them consider how that the Lord Jesus was at that moment within, having a hearing before the chief of the priests; whilst the Apostle Peter was outside, and down in the hall, sitting at one time with the servants at the fire, at another time standing, as the most accurate and consistent narrative of the evangelists shows.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: It cannot therefore be said that it was with His bodily eyes that the Lord turned and looked upon him by a visible and apparent admonition.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: That, then, which is described in the words, “The Lord turned and looked upon Peter,” was effected internally; it was wrought in the mind, wrought in the will.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: In mercy the Lord silently and secretly approached, touched the heart, recalled the memory of the past, with His own internal grace visited Peter, stirred and brought out into external tears the feelings of his inner man.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Behold in what manner God is present with His help to our wills and actions; behold how “He worketh in us both to will and to do.”
